text:
Record the strategic direction and backlog (L0-L5) that make lume capable of supporting its first real consumer: tredl, an autonomous-operator layer built on top of lume. This is a ROADMAP/PLANNING workstream under a human operator - it captures reasoning and lays out work; it does NOT implement any L-item or change lume's behaviour.

WHY. lume is a deterministic, operator-gated iteration loop where the operator is today a human. tredl (treadle, in the loom->lume lineage) is the same loop with Claude Code as the operator for the per-iteration gates, demoting the human to sprint boundaries (define the goal, review the delivered PRs). tredl uses lume; tredl never modifies lume - that would break lume's operator-in-the-loop contract. Instead tredl emits discoveries (capability gaps found in real use) that flow back into lume's backlog. The reasoning is recorded into the repo on purpose so an adversarial reviewer can see that unbuilt capability is staged roadmap serving demonstrated demand (tredl), not scope creep.

WHAT THIS WORKSTREAM IS. (1) A discovery iteration capturing the direction and reasoning, flagging the unverified premises (DoD write-only, no parent field on workstreams) as assumptions to confirm in source before building. (2) A planning iteration recording the backlog L0-L5 as plan items, in order, preserving L5's deferred/discovery-triggered status. (3) Logged decisions: in-repo discovery channel; strict machine-verifiable acceptance for the consumer; lume owns the template mechanism while the consumer owns the intelligence.

WHAT THIS WORKSTREAM IS NOT. Not an engine change. No L-item is implemented here. lume's focus and operator-in-the-loop principle are unchanged. The L-items, when scheduled, each run as their own workstream led by a discovery iteration that verifies its assumptions first.

DONE-WHEN. (a) A discovery iteration records the tredl direction, the feedback cycle, the autonomy ladder (lume->tredl->Jaqard), and explicitly flags the unverified premises as assumptions. (b) A planning iteration records L0-L5 as ordered plan items, L5 tagged deferred/discovery-triggered. (c) The locked decisions are logged via lume decide. (d) The .lume/ state is committed on a branch and merged via PR; no engine code changed. (e) No L-item is built and lume's behaviour is unchanged.
title: Enable autonomous operation (tredl support) and delivery hardening
